title: Analytics
journey: Schools already generate plenty of data — uploads, exports, gradebooks, attendance — but it rarely turns into a decision-ready view without someone manually pulling a spreadsheet together. Analytics takes the same underlying tasks, reports, attendance signals, and imports Mosaic already handles, and turns them into headline views leaders can actually act on. This is explicitly a later-stage feature: the first release focuses on getting the operational foundations right first.

subfeatures.name: Upload parser
subfeatures.note: Early groundwork can read a results file and map its columns to standard fields; the full upload workflow is still being shaped, with decisions about how data is kept and sourced to settle first.

subfeatures.name: Headline summary cards
subfeatures.note: Cohort, subject, and student-level summaries at a glance.

subfeatures.name: Task & workflow metrics
subfeatures.note: Operational analytics derived from task and workflow completion.

subfeatures.name: Risk & intervention signals
subfeatures.note: Combining attendance, grades, and task data to flag students who may need support.

subfeatures.name: Source & freshness metadata
subfeatures.note: Showing where a number came from and how current it is, before it's trusted.

testing.fixtureNote: The shared test data set still has no analytics entries, but an outcomes register now exists for this feature — mainly to say plainly that analytics is pre-release, and to pin down the few pieces that do exist today. Test data will be added once the upload format is settled, drawing on the same reporting data other features use.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: Analytics is clearly marked as not yet available
expectedOutcomes.expected: The home screen shows Analytics as coming soon, and there is no analytics page to open yet — nothing suggests a live feature.
expectedOutcomes.enforcedBy: Documented gap — no automated check yet; planned alongside the first analytics build

expectedOutcomes.criterion: Uploaded results columns map to standard fields
expectedOutcomes.expected: A results file whose column headings use common naming variations still maps onto the standard fields, and files missing required columns are caught before anything is shown downstream.
expectedOutcomes.enforcedBy: Documented gap — no automated check yet; planned alongside the first analytics build

expectedOutcomes.expected: The rule that analytics reads approved summary data rather than raw student records is a stated design commitment; it becomes verifiable the moment the first analytics view ships.
expectedOutcomes.enforcedBy: Documented design rule — verifiable once the first analytics view exists
